user.title: Freematics ONE+ – Connection Type
user.description: Choose how your Freematics ONE+ device will connect to the internet to send data to Home Assistant.

wifi.title: Freematics ONE+ – WiFi Credentials
wifi.description: Enter the WiFi network credentials that the Freematics ONE+ should connect to.

cellular.title: Freematics ONE+ – Cellular / SIM
cellular.description: Enter the APN for your cellular provider. Leave blank to use the default APN. The SIM PIN is only required if your SIM card has a PIN lock.

webhook.title: Freematics ONE+ – Webhook / Firmware Settings
webhook.description:
A unique webhook has been generated for your device.

Copy the settings below into your firmware configuration:

**SERVER_HOST**: `{ha_host}`
**SERVER_PORT**: `443`
**HA_WEBHOOK_ID**: `{webhook_id}`

Full webhook URL (for reference):
`{webhook_url}`

You can edit the Webhook ID below if you want to use a custom value.

device.title: Freematics ONE+ – Device Model
device.description:
Select your Freematics ONE+ hardware model. This helps tailor the recommended firmware settings.

- **Model A**: WiFi + Bluetooth, no cellular module
- **Model B**: WiFi + Bluetooth + 4G LTE cellular
- **Model H**: WiFi only (no Bluetooth, no cellular)

Detailed hardware specifications at [freematics.com](https://freematics.com).

advanced.title: Freematics ONE+ – Operating Mode
advanced.description:
Choose how the device should operate after flashing. This controls which firmware features are enabled.

**Telelogger** *(recommended for Home Assistant users)*: Pushes telemetry to the HA webhook. Keep HTTPD and BLE *disabled* to free ~104 KB of heap for the TLS connection — this prevents the `MBEDTLS_ERR_SSL_ALLOC_FAILED (-32512)` error.

**Datalogger**: Enables the built-in HTTP server (HTTPD) on port 80, exposing `/api/live`, `/api/info`, and related REST endpoints for local network access. Useful without Home Assistant or alongside a local dashboard.

**BLE** is independent of both modes. Enable it only if you use the Freematics Controller App over Bluetooth.

**Intervals**: leave at `0` to keep firmware defaults (~1000 ms / 120 s).

flash.title: Freematics ONE+ – Flash Method
flash.description:
Choose how to flash the firmware onto the device.

**WiFi OTA** *(recommended)*: The device must be powered on and reachable on your network, or in AP mode (connect to WiFi network **{ap_ssid}** with password **{ap_password}**, then enter `192.168.4.1` as Device IP). Works regardless of where Home Assistant is hosted.

**Serial USB (HA server)**: Runs `esptool` on the Home Assistant server. Only works if the Freematics ONE+ is connected via USB **directly to the HA server**.

**Serial USB (your computer)**: If the device is connected to the computer where your browser is running, use the [Browser Flasher]({flasher_url}) instead. It uses the Web Serial API (Chrome/Edge 89+) and requires no installation.

After completing setup, use the **Flash Firmware** buttons in the integration to start the flash process.

For detailed instructions see the [documentation](https://github.com/northpower25/Freematics/blob/master/docs/README.md#firmware-flashing).

abort.already_configured: This webhook ID is already configured.

init.title: Freematics ONE+ – Update Settings
init.description: Update the device settings. Changes are applied on the next flash or when you press **Send Config to Device**.
